label: Penis
quick_take: Penile cancer is a developing-country, low-circumcision cancer that has been falling almost everywhere — but *not* in Switzerland and parts of Eastern Europe, where it is creeping up. Brazil Recife, Jamaica Kingston, and Uganda are the persistent global hotspots.

findings.title: Brazil Recife men, vol III: ASR 9.54 and vol V: 7.94
findings.text: roughly 10–11× the world median. One of the most striking historical South American rates.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Brazil Recife men, vol III: ASR 9.54 and vol V: 7.94

findings.title: Uganda Gulu and Zimbabwe Bulawayo African men sit above 5 in vol XII
findings.text: African penile cancer remains ~10× world median (Zimbabwe Bulawayo African n=56 over the full period; Uganda Gulu single-volume n=21). Pop_warning for Bulawayo vol XII. Level is credible; Latin American registries have largely converged to global levels.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Uganda Gulu and Zimbabwe Bulawayo African men sit above 5 in vol XII

findings.title: China Shanghai and Hong Kong men: clean U-shapes
findings.text: Shanghai 1.58 → 0 → 0.31, Hong Kong 1.95 → 0 → 0.37. Intermediate volumes have near-zero cases for these registries; the U is directionally suggestive but the bottom of the U is at the limit of what CI5 can resolve for this rare cancer. Improving hygiene / early circumcision increase during urban transition is the usual hypothesis.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — China Shanghai and Hong Kong men: clean U-shapes

findings.text: Small counts everywhere — treat all % changes cautiously.
CI5's penis code (C60) includes both invasive SCC and in-situ; some of the Swiss rise may reflect better capture of Tis.
Revisit Brazil Recife's 1970s–80s readings with an ethnographic lens: still one of the highest per-capita historical rates for any penile cancer population.
